Amazon's own page displayed that you would get a $10 bonus for reloading $100 on a gift card for the first time, however, they had an expiration date listed on the promotion showing "Expires on October" and it's already November... what gives? Well I thought forget that then, but as I scrolled through Amazon the promo kept popping up everywhere, well I finally I was going to buy something for about $100 and thought I could try the reload to see if the expiration date was wrong or what, I was going to spend the money anyway. Reloaded the card, and then had to wait several minutes with no confirmation that the card was even reloaded' then I wasn't able to even see the credit given to the gift card for several minutes after that - like ok, where is it? Finally showed up, and later after that I got my $10 credit, but damn! What a convoluted mess.

I had been giving Omaha Steak gift cards for years at Christmas. I could include them with the Christmas cards for people I wouldn't see during the holidays. It has since been brought to my attention that the company now has such a high threshold for free shipping and a high shipping rate that it is now cost prohibitive to purchase from them online. Even when given a gift card! I'm now forcing friends and family to spend more just to get a gift! If you can find an actual store, maybe you're alright but mail order is out of the question. It used to be fun. People would buy special packages of steaks or burgers. Or try something out of the ordinary. Now it seems its more of a hassle to find something you'll like and not spend more then the gift cards! The weight of the meats is small, the postage is high and adding heavy potatoes to a combo isn't really "special". I will NOT continue buying these in the future.

Initially, i thought i had a bogus card, as Spotify would just not accept the PIN. I already had a premium account, and was trying to use this card to "recharge" it, similar to how the Netflix gift cards work (they just add the $ to your account whether you're subscribed or not).
This card does not work that way, however. So, at the advice of amazon support, i cancelled my spotify premium account, and waited for it to expire. When it did, i went to my account, redeemed the PIN, and it worked like a charm.
So it works as advertised, if not how i expected, and it's nice to save a couple of bucks for the year.

I know we all already buy everything from Amazon, and they basically own the rights to me. They want me on that familiar checkout page 5-10 times a day.... but seriously, why force your "gift card reload" process through the same process as making a purchase. It is NOT intuitive compared to "add funds" to a digital balance on basically any other consumer app/service (e.g., Starbucks). While my mouse was hovering over the button to complete my purchase, I had a moment of genuine pause wondering if I had been phished into clicking the wrong link and was about to order a fake product listing called "Amazon Reload"... that's how confusing this is.

To their credit, the reload process worked, and I got my $12 loaded within 5 minutes. Two stars for it technically working.

While the presentation is really pretty, great for a wedding or bridal shower, the card itself is thin paper and totally flimsy feeling. Not like the normal Amazon gift card plastic. It also does not stay inside the box. So when I first opened mine, there was no card in there when I went to check and opeend the drawer. Just an empty space in the silk lining. I thought it was stolen somehow or a defect. But I did hear something rattling around still so I had to (not easily) take the box apart and find the card behind it. And then felt how flimsy it was. I was also skeptical that someone stole the real one and put in a fake somehow...but now reading other reviews,  that's just how it comes. So I guess you sacrifice card design for box design in this case.

These gift cards are very convenient as thank you gift to the speakers at our yearly event. They were delivered within 2 days of purchase.
They are very convenient to the speakers too as they can use them to buy nearly anything they want on Amazon.com
We usually give two $50 cards per speaker, but it doesn't look good to hand them bare, so we hand them in an envelope we have to buy separately. That's where there is some inconvenience: we have to find envelopes of the proper size somewhere.
It would be nice and a 5 star convenience if Amazon.com would provide the option of buying the cards with matching envelopes or sleeves. That option was not available when I bought the cards.
I will still buy another set for our next year event.

François Cartier
SF-DAMA, treasurer

My prime membership expires in January. had the same type of HORRIBLE experience this past summer. I bought a grill cover and the seller sat on it for 10 days before he finally shipped it (said his mail delivery person wouldn't take the package because of an ongoing problem with his account). The entire time he's got the package ready to go by his front door, he never checked to be certain it was the right cover for my grill. After it arrived 2 weeks later, it was WAY TOO SMALL for my grill. Needless to say, he didn't get a very good review from me. Then the seller starts threatening me with negative feedback on my business profile online. Fast forward to the same thing this guy said happened. Nobody in Customer service is ever on the same page. In my opinion, they just want you to be happy and move on without ever really understanding the main problem that will continue. I love the entire way Prime is supposed to work, but after reading many experiences like mine for different reasons, the problem remains the same. I have a feeling that one day the wheels may really fall off at AMAZON, the customer service here is like the way big banks used to be - TOO BIG TO FAIL...
